mission.title: kubeflow: Dashboard's contributors section doesn't show all the namespaces I own
mission.description: Dashboard's contributors section doesn't show all the namespaces I own. This issue affects 8+ users.

steps.title: Identify kubeflow troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kubeflow deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=kubeflow
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Dashboard's contributors section doesn't show all the namespaces I own

steps.title: Check current Namespace configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubeflow resources:
```bash
kubectl get namespace -A
kubectl describe namespace <name> -n <namespace>
```
**What steps did you take and what happened:**
1. I set up a Kubeflow 0.7 cluster and get a `gongyuan` namespace.
2. I created a profile `demo` which also marks me as the owner.
3. However, in dashboa

steps.title: Apply the fix for Dashboard's contributors section doesn't show all the namesp
steps.description:
Estimated efforts is 1~1.5 weeks, we need the relevant updates to the 2 other components, namespace selector (trivial if any), registration flow (also perhaps trivial), and tests

### Some notes for how to develop
* First time only:
```
npm i
make build-local
npm run build
```
* Run the commands in 
```yaml
npm i
make build-local
npm run build
```

steps.title: Confirm Dashboard's contributors section doesn't show all  is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: Estimated efforts is 1~1.5 weeks, we need the relevant updates to the 2 other components, namespace selector (trivial if any), registration flow (also perhaps trivial), and tests

### Some notes for how to develop
* First time only:
```
npm i
make build-local
npm run build
```
* Run the commands in two separate terminals: `npm run dev` and `kubectl port-forward svc/profiles-kfam 8081:8081`

### To. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
codeSnippets:
npm i
make build-local
npm run build

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubeflow installed or the issue environment reproducible.
